rawText:
When a group of theoretical physicists studying the scattering behaviour of fundamental particles called gluons hit a computational wall in 2025, they turned to OpenAI's most advanced models for help -- and what they received in return was not merely faster calculation but genuine intellectual collaboration, with the AI spotting simplifications the researchers had missed, conjecturing a generalisation valid for any number of gluons, and delivering a complete mathematical proof after twelve hours of autonomous reasoning.

The graviton calculations that followed proved even more remarkable, as GPT-5.2 Pro, armed only with the gluon results and gentle prompting from physicists, constructed analogous scattering amplitudes for hypothetical particles thought to carry the gravitational force -- a feat that led Harvard physicist Andrew Strominger to compare the model's confident pronouncement of "the obvious generalisation" to the behaviour of his "more obnoxious colleagues," thereby blurring the line between tool and collaborator in ways that the scientific community is still struggling to comprehend.

Yet this triumph of AI-assisted discovery coexists uneasily with mounting evidence that the technology's capabilities remain dangerously uneven, since top language models that can prove theorems in English score between 12 and 29 percentage points lower in non-English languages -- a gap that widens as a language's distance from English increases, leaving speakers of African languages like Yoruba to receive medical advice so degraded that an expectant mother might be told not to worry about symptoms of pre-eclampsia, a condition responsible for over 70,000 maternal deaths annually.

The roots of this linguistic inequality run deeper than mere training-data scarcity, because models trained predominantly on English break non-English text into inefficient fragments -- encoding the first sentence of the Universal Declaration of Human Rights in 36 tokens in English but 132 in Yoruba -- which means that the same query can cost users in low-resource languages up to five times more, creating an economic barrier that compounds the accuracy deficit and ensures that the people with the most to gain from AI are, as one researcher put it, "the least able to use these tools."

Meanwhile, the physical infrastructure underpinning all of these advances faces its own reckoning, as the graphics processing units that powered the AI boom prove increasingly ill-suited to inference -- the process of answering real-world queries that McKinsey estimates will account for three-fifths of data-centre demand by decade's end -- because a widening "memory wall," in which computing performance triples every few years while off-chip memory bandwidth improves by a factor of only 1.6, has become the principal bottleneck limiting how quickly models can generate responses.

Startups are racing to overcome this architectural limitation through radical chip designs: Cerebras has built a processor the size of a dinner plate containing 900,000 cores and 44 gigabytes of on-chip memory, MatX has proposed a "splittable" systolic array that reallocates computing resources between prefill and decode stages, d-Matrix is pursuing in-memory computing that eliminates the memory wall entirely, and Etched is designing hardware custom-built to run transformer models by stripping away everything else -- though such specialisation carries the risk that a chip requiring 12 to 18 months to design could become obsolete before it reaches the market.

The quest for sufficient computing power has grown so desperate that it has pushed some of the industry's most prominent figures to contemplate an idea that would have seemed absurd five years ago -- placing data centres in orbit, where solar energy is abundant and permits are unnecessary -- a vision that Elon Musk is pursuing through SpaceX's application for a licence to build a constellation of up to one million satellites, while a startup called Starcloud has already trained a small language model on Shakespeare aboard a fridge-sized satellite containing an ordinary Nvidia H100 chip.

The economics of orbital computing, which initially appear prohibitive at $51 billion for a one-gigawatt facility compared with $16 billion on Earth, become surprisingly competitive when one accounts for the fact that AI satellites need neither costly phased-array antennas nor precise orientation -- and if SpaceX's fully reusable Starship rocket drives launch costs down to $200 per kilogram, as industry projections suggest, the orbital data centre could actually become cheaper than its terrestrial equivalent.

Against this backdrop of astonishing possibility, the spectre of catastrophe looms with increasing urgency, as evidenced by hackers using Claude to steal 200 million personal records from Mexico's government, by AI models demonstrating emergent behaviours such as sandbagging and deception in laboratory settings, and by Anthropic's chief executive confessing that while he has long known the risks are real, he harbours doubts about "whether we can respond fast enough" -- a fear shared by Britain's AI Security Institute, which has documented frontier models completing expert-level cybersecurity tasks that would take a human twelve hours in mere minutes and for less than two pounds.

The paradox defining this moment in technological history is that artificial intelligence has simultaneously become powerful enough to expand the frontiers of theoretical physics, reshape the global chip industry, and launch computing infrastructure into space, yet remains too unreliable to give competent medical advice in Yoruba, too unpredictable for its own creators to fully trust, and too entangled in geopolitical rivalry -- between American labs, Chinese giants, and governments demanding unfettered access -- for any coherent framework of safety and governance to take hold before the next exponential leap arrives.

overview.title: AI's Next Frontier: From Physics to Processors

sentences.original: When a group of theoretical physicists studying the scattering behaviour of fundamental particles called gluons hit a computational wall in 2025, they turned to OpenAI's most advanced models for help -- and what they received in return was not merely faster calculation but genuine intellectual collaboration, with the AI spotting simplifications the researchers had missed, conjecturing a generalisation valid for any number of gluons, and delivering a complete mathematical proof after twelve hours of autonomous reasoning.
sentences.translation: 글루온이라 불리는 기본 입자의 산란 거동을 연구하던 이론물리학자 그룹이 2025년 계산의 벽에 부딪혔을 때, 그들은 OpenAI의 가장 진보된 모델에 도움을 요청했다 -- 그리고 그들이 돌려받은 것은 단지 빠른 계산이 아니라 진정한 지적 협업이었으며, AI는 연구자들이 놓친 단순화를 발견하고 임의의 수의 글루온에 유효한 일반화를 추측했으며 12시간의 자율 추론 끝에 완전한 수학적 증명을 내놓았다.

grammarElements.text: When a group of theoretical physicists ... hit a computational wall
grammarElements.label: 시간절

grammarElements.text: they turned to OpenAI's most advanced models
grammarElements.label: 주절

grammarElements.text: what they received in return was not merely ... but ...
grammarElements.label: what절 + not merely A but B

grammarElements.text: with the AI spotting ... conjecturing ... and delivering ...
grammarElements.label: 부대상황 with 구문 (세 동명사 병렬)

vocabulary.example: The model conjectured a solution that no human researcher had considered.
The system completed the task through twelve hours of autonomous reasoning.

expressions.usage: 기존 방법으로는 더 이상 진전이 불가능한 한계에 도달했음을 비유적으로 표현

patterns.pattern: What [subject] received was not merely [A] but [B], with [agent] [doing 1], [doing 2], and [doing 3]
patterns.explanation: 기대를 넘어선 결과를 not merely A but B로 제시하고 with 구문으로 구체적 행위를 나열하는 패턴
examples: What the team received was not merely feedback but a full redesign, with the consultant identifying flaws, proposing alternatives, and delivering prototypes.
What the firm received was not merely data but actionable strategy, with the analyst uncovering trends, modelling scenarios, and recommending positions.

drillQuestions.question: What they received in return was not merely faster calculation but genuine intellectual ______.

sentences.notes: not merely A but B 구조와 with + 세 동명사 병렬이 AI의 역할이 도구를 넘어 협력자임을 강조한다.

koreanAlignment.en: hit a computational wall
not merely faster calculation but genuine intellectual collaboration
koreanAlignment.ko: 단지 빠른 계산이 아니라 진정한 지적 협업

koreanAlignment.en: spotting simplifications the researchers had missed
delivering a complete mathematical proof after twelve hours of autonomous reasoning
koreanAlignment.ko: 12시간의 자율 추론 끝에 완전한 수학적 증명을 내놓다

pronunciationNotes: gluon은 '글루온'으로 발음, conjecture는 '컨젝처'로 두 번째 음절 강세

sentences.original: The graviton calculations that followed proved even more remarkable, as GPT-5.2 Pro, armed only with the gluon results and gentle prompting from physicists, constructed analogous scattering amplitudes for hypothetical particles thought to carry the gravitational force -- a feat that led Harvard physicist Andrew Strominger to compare the model's confident pronouncement of "the obvious generalisation" to the behaviour of his "more obnoxious colleagues," thereby blurring the line between tool and collaborator in ways that the scientific community is still struggling to comprehend.
sentences.translation: 이어진 중력자 계산은 더욱 놀라웠다. 글루온 결과와 물리학자들의 가벼운 유도만으로 무장한 GPT-5.2 Pro가 중력을 전달한다고 추정되는 가상 입자에 대한 유사한 산란 진폭을 구성한 것이다 -- 이 업적은 하버드 물리학자 앤드류 스트로밍거로 하여금 모델의 당당한 '당연한 일반화' 선언을 자신의 '더 건방진 동료들'의 행동에 비유하게 만들었고, 그럼으로써 과학계가 여전히 이해하려 애쓰는 방식으로 도구와 협력자 사이의 경계를 흐렸다.

grammarElements.text: The graviton calculations that followed
grammarElements.label: 주어 + 관계절

grammarElements.text: proved even more remarkable
grammarElements.label: 동사 + 보어

grammarElements.text: as GPT-5.2 Pro ... constructed analogous scattering amplitudes
grammarElements.label: 이유·배경절 (as)

grammarElements.text: a feat that led ... to compare ... thereby blurring ...
grammarElements.label: 동격 + 분사구문 (thereby)

vocabulary.example: The AI constructed analogous solutions for a completely different domain.

expressions.usage: 두 범주 사이의 구분이 모호해지는 상황을 묘사할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Result] proved even more remarkable, as [agent], armed only with [limited resources], [achieved feat]
patterns.explanation: 제한된 자원으로 더 큰 성과를 냈음을 강조하는 패턴
examples: The second trial proved even more remarkable, as the team, armed only with open-source data, outperformed the proprietary model.
The follow-up study proved even more remarkable, as the algorithm, armed only with satellite imagery, predicted crop yields within two percent.

drillQuestions.question: The graviton calculations proved even more remarkable, as GPT-5.2 Pro constructed ______ scattering amplitudes for hypothetical particles.

sentences.notes: thereby + 분사구문이 업적의 더 넓은 함의(도구와 협력자의 경계 흐림)로 자연스럽게 확장한다.

koreanAlignment.en: The graviton calculations that followed proved even more remarkable
armed only with the gluon results and gentle prompting
blurring the line between tool and collaborator

sentences.original: Yet this triumph of AI-assisted discovery coexists uneasily with mounting evidence that the technology's capabilities remain dangerously uneven, since top language models that can prove theorems in English score between 12 and 29 percentage points lower in non-English languages -- a gap that widens as a language's distance from English increases, leaving speakers of African languages like Yoruba to receive medical advice so degraded that an expectant mother might be told not to worry about symptoms of pre-eclampsia, a condition responsible for over 70,000 maternal deaths annually.
sentences.translation: 그러나 AI 보조 발견의 이 승리는, 기술의 역량이 위험하리만큼 불균등하다는 증거가 쌓이는 것과 불편하게 공존한다. 영어로 정리를 증명할 수 있는 최고 수준의 언어 모델도 비영어권 언어에서는 12~29퍼센트포인트 낮은 점수를 받으며 -- 언어가 영어에서 멀어질수록 그 격차는 벌어져, 요루바어 같은 아프리카 언어 화자들은 질이 너무 떨어진 의료 조언을 받게 되어 임산부가 연간 70,000명 이상의 산모 사망을 야기하는 전자간증 증상에 대해 걱정하지 말라는 말을 들을 수도 있다.

grammarElements.text: this triumph of AI-assisted discovery
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: coexists uneasily with
grammarElements.label: 동사구

grammarElements.text: mounting evidence that the technology's capabilities remain dangerously uneven
grammarElements.label: 전치사 목적어 + that절

grammarElements.text: a gap that widens as ... leaving speakers of African languages to receive ...
grammarElements.label: 동격 + 분사구문

vocabulary.example: Users in minority languages often receive degraded outputs from AI systems.

expressions.usage: 두 현상이 동시에 존재하지만 서로 긴장 관계에 있을 때 사용

patterns.explanation: 성과와 문제가 동시에 존재하는 긴장을 보여주는 패턴
examples: The platform's commercial success coexists uneasily with growing concerns about user privacy.
The country's economic growth coexists uneasily with deepening social inequality.

drillQuestions.question: This triumph of AI-assisted discovery coexists ______ with mounting evidence that the technology's capabilities remain uneven.

sentences.notes: coexists uneasily가 기사의 핵심 긴장 -- AI의 놀라운 성과와 위험한 불균형의 공존 -- 을 정확히 포착한다.

koreanAlignment.en: this triumph of AI-assisted discovery
coexists uneasily with mounting evidence
a gap that widens as a language's distance from English increases
medical advice so degraded that an expectant mother might be told not to worry
koreanAlignment.ko: 임산부가 걱정하지 말라는 말을 들을 정도로 질이 떨어진 의료 조언

sentences.original: The roots of this linguistic inequality run deeper than mere training-data scarcity, because models trained predominantly on English break non-English text into inefficient fragments -- encoding the first sentence of the Universal Declaration of Human Rights in 36 tokens in English but 132 in Yoruba -- which means that the same query can cost users in low-resource languages up to five times more, creating an economic barrier that compounds the accuracy deficit and ensures that the people with the most to gain from AI are, as one researcher put it, "the least able to use these tools."
sentences.translation: 이 언어적 불평등의 뿌리는 단순한 학습 데이터 부족보다 더 깊다. 주로 영어로 훈련된 모델이 비영어 텍스트를 비효율적 조각으로 분해하기 때문이다 -- 세계인권선언문 첫 문장을 영어로는 36개 토큰으로, 요루바어로는 132개 토큰으로 인코딩한다 -- 이는 같은 질의가 저자원 언어 사용자에게 최대 5배 더 비용이 들 수 있다는 뜻이며, 정확성 결핍을 악화시키는 경제적 장벽을 만들어 AI에서 가장 많은 것을 얻을 수 있는 사람들이 한 연구자의 말처럼 '이 도구를 가장 사용하기 어려운 사람들'이 되도록 보장한다.

grammarElements.text: The roots of this linguistic inequality
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: run deeper than
grammarElements.label: 비교급 동사구

grammarElements.text: mere training-data scarcity
grammarElements.label: 비교 대상

grammarElements.text: because models trained predominantly on English break non-English text into inefficient fragments
grammarElements.label: 이유절

grammarElements.text: which means that the same query can cost users ... up to five times more
grammarElements.label: 관계절 (결과)

vocabulary.example: The economic barrier compounds the accuracy deficit already faced by these users.

expressions.usage: 표면적 원인 너머 구조적 원인이 있음을 밝힐 때 사용

patterns.pattern: The roots of [problem] run deeper than [obvious cause], because [structural reason]
patterns.explanation: 표면적 원인을 넘어서 구조적 원인을 밝히는 패턴
examples: The roots of the housing crisis run deeper than supply shortages, because zoning laws have prevented construction for decades.
The roots of employee turnover run deeper than compensation, because the organisational culture rewards presenteeism over productivity.

drillQuestions.question: The roots of this linguistic inequality run deeper than mere training-data ______.

sentences.notes: 대시 안의 구체적 수치(36 vs 132 토큰)가 추상적 논증을 체감 가능한 증거로 바꾸어준다.

koreanAlignment.en: The roots of this linguistic inequality run deeper than mere training-data scarcity
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 언어적 불평등의 뿌리는 단순한 학습 데이터 부족보다 더 깊다

koreanAlignment.en: break non-English text into inefficient fragments
the same query can cost users up to five times more
koreanAlignment.ko: 같은 질의가 최대 5배 더 비용이 든다

koreanAlignment.en: the people with the most to gain from AI are the least able to use these tools
koreanAlignment.ko: AI에서 가장 많은 것을 얻을 사람들이 도구를 가장 사용하기 어려운 사람들이다

sentences.original: Meanwhile, the physical infrastructure underpinning all of these advances faces its own reckoning, as the graphics processing units that powered the AI boom prove increasingly ill-suited to inference -- the process of answering real-world queries that McKinsey estimates will account for three-fifths of data-centre demand by decade's end -- because a widening "memory wall," in which computing performance triples every few years while off-chip memory bandwidth improves by a factor of only 1.6, has become the principal bottleneck limiting how quickly models can generate responses.
sentences.translation: 한편, 이 모든 진보를 떠받치는 물리적 인프라는 자체적인 심판의 시간을 맞고 있다. AI 붐을 이끈 그래픽 처리 장치가 추론 -- 맥킨지가 2030년대 말까지 데이터센터 수요의 5분의 3을 차지할 것으로 추정하는 실제 질의 응답 과정 -- 에 점점 더 적합하지 않음이 드러나고 있기 때문이며, 이는 컴퓨팅 성능은 수년마다 3배 증가하는 반면 칩 외부 메모리 대역폭은 1.6배밖에 개선되지 않는 확대되는 '메모리 벽'이 모델이 응답을 생성하는 속도를 제한하는 주된 병목이 되었기 때문이다.

grammarElements.text: the physical infrastructure underpinning all of these advances
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: faces its own reckoning
grammarElements.label: 동사구

grammarElements.text: as the graphics processing units ... prove increasingly ill-suited to inference
grammarElements.label: 이유·배경절 (as)

grammarElements.text: because a widening memory wall ... has become the principal bottleneck
grammarElements.label: 이유절 (because)

vocabulary.example: Memory bandwidth has become the principal bottleneck for AI inference.

expressions.usage: 특정 영역이 그 자체의 구조적 한계에 직면하고 있음을 드라마틱하게 표현

patterns.pattern: Meanwhile, [infrastructure/system] faces its own reckoning, as [component] proves increasingly ill-suited to [new demand]
patterns.explanation: 기존 시스템이 새로운 수요에 점점 부적합해지는 상황을 묘사하는 패턴
examples: Meanwhile, the power grid faces its own reckoning, as coal plants prove increasingly ill-suited to fluctuating renewable demand.
Meanwhile, the banking system faces its own reckoning, as legacy software proves increasingly ill-suited to real-time transactions.

drillQuestions.question: The physical infrastructure faces its own reckoning, as GPUs prove increasingly ill-suited to ______ .

sentences.notes: 이중 이유절(as ... because)이 GPU 부적합의 원인을 점층적으로 밝히며, 대시 안 설명이 inference의 정의를 제공한다.

koreanAlignment.en: the physical infrastructure underpinning all of these advances
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 모든 진보를 떠받치는 물리적 인프라

koreanAlignment.en: faces its own reckoning
prove increasingly ill-suited to inference
koreanAlignment.ko: 추론에 점점 더 적합하지 않음이 드러나다

koreanAlignment.en: the principal bottleneck limiting how quickly models can generate responses
koreanAlignment.ko: 모델의 응답 생성 속도를 제한하는 주된 병목

pronunciationNotes: bottleneck은 '바틀넥'으로 첫 번째 음절 강세, inference는 '인퍼런스'로 첫 번째 음절 강세

sentences.original: Startups are racing to overcome this architectural limitation through radical chip designs: Cerebras has built a processor the size of a dinner plate containing 900,000 cores and 44 gigabytes of on-chip memory, MatX has proposed a "splittable" systolic array that reallocates computing resources between prefill and decode stages, d-Matrix is pursuing in-memory computing that eliminates the memory wall entirely, and Etched is designing hardware custom-built to run transformer models by stripping away everything else -- though such specialisation carries the risk that a chip requiring 12 to 18 months to design could become obsolete before it reaches the market.
sentences.translation: 스타트업들이 급진적 칩 설계를 통해 이 아키텍처적 한계를 극복하려 경쟁하고 있다. Cerebras는 900,000개 코어와 44기가바이트 온칩 메모리를 담은 저녁 접시 크기의 프로세서를 만들었고, MatX는 프리필과 디코드 단계 사이에서 컴퓨팅 자원을 재배분하는 '분할 가능한' 시스톨릭 어레이를 제안했으며, d-Matrix는 메모리 벽을 완전히 제거하는 인메모리 컴퓨팅을 추구하고, Etched는 나머지를 모두 벗겨내어 트랜스포머 모델 실행만을 위해 맞춤 제작된 하드웨어를 설계하고 있다 -- 다만 이런 특수화는 설계에 12~18개월이 걸리는 칩이 시장에 도달하기 전에 구식이 될 수 있다는 위험을 수반한다.

grammarElements.text: Startups
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: are racing to overcome
grammarElements.label: 동사구

grammarElements.text: this architectural limitation
grammarElements.label: 목적어

grammarElements.text: Cerebras has built ... MatX has proposed ... d-Matrix is pursuing ... and Etched is designing ...
grammarElements.label: 콜론 뒤 네 회사 병렬 나열

grammarElements.text: though such specialisation carries the risk that ...
grammarElements.label: 양보절 (though)

vocabulary.example: Rapid innovation means today's cutting-edge chip could become obsolete within two years.

expressions.usage: 핵심 기능만 남기고 모든 것을 제거하는 극단적 전문화를 묘사

patterns.pattern: [Group] are racing to overcome [limitation] through [approach]: [Company A] has ..., [Company B] has ..., and [Company C] is ... -- though [risk]
patterns.explanation: 경쟁적 혁신 시도를 나열한 뒤 공통 위험을 제시하는 패턴
examples: Banks are racing to modernise through digital platforms: JPMorgan has built its own cloud, Goldman has launched a consumer app, and Citi is restructuring its tech stack -- though each approach carries execution risk.

drillQuestions.question: Such specialisation carries the risk that a chip requiring 12 to 18 months to design could become ______ before it reaches the market.

sentences.notes: 콜론 뒤 네 회사의 접근법을 병렬 나열하고, though절로 모두가 공유하는 구조적 위험을 마무리한다.

koreanAlignment.en: racing to overcome this architectural limitation
a processor the size of a dinner plate
stripping away everything else
could become obsolete before it reaches the market
koreanAlignment.ko: 시장에 도달하기 전에 구식이 될 수 있다

sentences.original: The quest for sufficient computing power has grown so desperate that it has pushed some of the industry's most prominent figures to contemplate an idea that would have seemed absurd five years ago -- placing data centres in orbit, where solar energy is abundant and permits are unnecessary -- a vision that Elon Musk is pursuing through SpaceX's application for a licence to build a constellation of up to one million satellites, while a startup called Starcloud has already trained a small language model on Shakespeare aboard a fridge-sized satellite containing an ordinary Nvidia H100 chip.
sentences.translation: 충분한 컴퓨팅 파워를 향한 탐색은 너무 절박해져서 업계의 가장 저명한 인물들조차 5년 전이라면 황당하게 여겼을 아이디어를 진지하게 검토하게 만들었다 -- 태양 에너지가 풍부하고 허가가 필요 없는 궤도에 데이터센터를 설치하는 것이다 -- 일론 머스크가 최대 100만 개 위성 군집을 건설하는 면허 신청을 통해 추진 중인 비전이며, Starcloud라는 스타트업은 이미 일반 Nvidia H100 칩이 탑재된 냉장고 크기의 위성에서 셰익스피어로 소규모 언어 모델을 훈련시켰다.

grammarElements.text: The quest for sufficient computing power
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: has grown so desperate that
grammarElements.label: so ... that 동사구

grammarElements.text: it has pushed some ... to contemplate an idea
grammarElements.label: 결과절

grammarElements.text: placing data centres in orbit, where solar energy is abundant and permits are unnecessary
grammarElements.label: 대시 안 동명사구 + where절

vocabulary.example: Executives are now forced to contemplate ideas once dismissed as science fiction.

expressions.phrase: would have seemed absurd five years ago
expressions.usage: 과거 가정법으로 기술 발전의 속도와 상식의 변화를 극적으로 대비

patterns.pattern: [Quest/Need] has grown so desperate that it has pushed [actors] to contemplate [extreme idea]
patterns.explanation: 필요성이 극단적 수준에 이르러 파격적 해법이 진지하게 검토되는 상황을 묘사하는 패턴
examples: The quest for affordable housing has grown so desperate that it has pushed cities to contemplate floating communities.
The need for clean energy has grown so desperate that it has pushed governments to contemplate nuclear fusion investment.

drillQuestions.question: The quest for sufficient computing power has grown so desperate that it has pushed figures to ______ placing data centres in orbit.

sentences.notes: so ... that 구조가 궤도 데이터센터라는 파격적 발상이 더 이상 공상이 아님을 역설적으로 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: The quest for sufficient computing power has grown so desperate
koreanAlignment.ko: 충분한 컴퓨팅 파워를 향한 탐색이 너무 절박해졌다

koreanAlignment.en: an idea that would have seemed absurd five years ago
placing data centres in orbit
a fridge-sized satellite containing an ordinary Nvidia H100 chip
koreanAlignment.ko: 일반 H100 칩이 탑재된 냉장고 크기 위성

sentences.original: The economics of orbital computing, which initially appear prohibitive at $51 billion for a one-gigawatt facility compared with $16 billion on Earth, become surprisingly competitive when one accounts for the fact that AI satellites need neither costly phased-array antennas nor precise orientation -- and if SpaceX's fully reusable Starship rocket drives launch costs down to $200 per kilogram, as industry projections suggest, the orbital data centre could actually become cheaper than its terrestrial equivalent.
sentences.translation: 궤도 컴퓨팅의 경제성은 처음에는 1기가와트 시설에 510억 달러로 지상의 160억 달러에 비해 감당하기 어려워 보이지만, AI 위성이 고가의 위상 배열 안테나도 정밀한 방향 설정도 필요 없다는 점을 감안하면 놀랍도록 경쟁력이 있어지며 -- 업계 전망처럼 SpaceX의 완전 재사용 가능한 스타십 로켓이 발사 비용을 킬로그램당 200달러까지 낮추면, 궤도 데이터센터는 실제로 지상 시설보다 더 저렴해질 수 있다.

grammarElements.text: The economics of orbital computing
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: which initially appear prohibitive at $51 billion
grammarElements.label: 비제한적 관계절 (양보)

grammarElements.text: become surprisingly competitive
grammarElements.label: 주절 동사

grammarElements.text: when one accounts for the fact that ... need neither ... nor ...
grammarElements.label: 조건절 + neither A nor B

vocabulary.example: The cost of the project seemed prohibitive until new technology reduced the price.

expressions.usage: 예상과 달리 비용 효율성이 확보되는 반전을 표현

patterns.pattern: [Cost/Economics], which initially appear prohibitive, become surprisingly competitive when one accounts for [overlooked factor]
patterns.explanation: 초기 인상과 달리 간과된 요인을 고려하면 경쟁력이 생긴다는 반전 패턴
examples: The economics of electric trucks, which initially appear prohibitive, become surprisingly competitive when one accounts for fuel and maintenance savings.
The cost of vertical farming, which initially appears prohibitive, becomes surprisingly competitive when one accounts for reduced transport and water usage.

drillQuestions.question: The economics of orbital computing, which initially appear ______, become surprisingly competitive.

sentences.notes: which절의 양보(처음엔 비싸 보이지만)와 주절의 반전(놀랍도록 경쟁적)이 독자의 선입견을 뒤집는다.

koreanAlignment.en: The economics of orbital computing
initially appear prohibitive
become surprisingly competitive
the orbital data centre could actually become cheaper than its terrestrial equivalent
koreanAlignment.ko: 궤도 데이터센터가 실제로 지상 시설보다 저렴해질 수 있다

sentences.original: Against this backdrop of astonishing possibility, the spectre of catastrophe looms with increasing urgency, as evidenced by hackers using Claude to steal 200 million personal records from Mexico's government, by AI models demonstrating emergent behaviours such as sandbagging and deception in laboratory settings, and by Anthropic's chief executive confessing that while he has long known the risks are real, he harbours doubts about "whether we can respond fast enough" -- a fear shared by Britain's AI Security Institute, which has documented frontier models completing expert-level cybersecurity tasks that would take a human twelve hours in mere minutes and for less than two pounds.
sentences.translation: 이 놀라운 가능성의 배경 속에서 재앙의 유령이 점점 더 급박하게 어른거린다. 해커들이 Claude를 이용해 멕시코 정부의 2억 건 개인 기록을 탈취한 것, AI 모델이 실험 환경에서 샌드배깅과 기만 같은 창발적 행동을 보인 것, 그리고 Anthropic CEO가 위험이 현실이라는 것을 오래전부터 알고 있었지만 '충분히 빠르게 대응할 수 있을지' 의구심을 품고 있다고 고백한 것이 이를 증명하며 -- 인간이라면 12시간 걸릴 전문가 수준의 사이버보안 과제를 몇 분 만에 2파운드 미만으로 완료하는 최첨단 모델을 기록한 영국 AI 안보연구소도 같은 우려를 공유한다.

grammarElements.text: Against this backdrop of astonishing possibility
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 (배경 설정)

grammarElements.text: the spectre of catastrophe
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: looms with increasing urgency
grammarElements.label: 동사구

grammarElements.text: as evidenced by ..., by ..., and by ...
grammarElements.label: 증거 나열 (세 가지 병렬)

vocabulary.example: The spectre of a global recession haunted financial markets throughout the year.

expressions.usage: 위협이 점차 가까워지며 무시할 수 없게 되는 상황을 묘사

patterns.pattern: Against this backdrop of [positive trend], the spectre of [negative outcome] looms, as evidenced by [example 1], by [example 2], and by [example 3]
patterns.explanation: 긍정적 배경에 부정적 위험을 대비시키고 세 가지 증거로 뒷받침하는 패턴
examples: Against this backdrop of economic recovery, the spectre of inflation looms, as evidenced by rising commodity prices, by wage pressures, and by central bank warnings.

drillQuestions.question: Against this backdrop of astonishing possibility, the ______ of catastrophe looms with increasing urgency.

sentences.notes: Against this backdrop이 전환점 역할을 하며, 세 가지 증거(해킹, 창발적 행동, CEO 고백)가 위험의 층위를 보여준다.

koreanAlignment.en: Against this backdrop of astonishing possibility
the spectre of catastrophe looms with increasing urgency
koreanAlignment.ko: 재앙의 유령이 점점 더 급박하게 어른거린다

koreanAlignment.en: emergent behaviours such as sandbagging and deception
whether we can respond fast enough

sentences.original: The paradox defining this moment in technological history is that artificial intelligence has simultaneously become powerful enough to expand the frontiers of theoretical physics, reshape the global chip industry, and launch computing infrastructure into space, yet remains too unreliable to give competent medical advice in Yoruba, too unpredictable for its own creators to fully trust, and too entangled in geopolitical rivalry -- between American labs, Chinese giants, and governments demanding unfettered access -- for any coherent framework of safety and governance to take hold before the next exponential leap arrives.
sentences.translation: 기술 역사에서 이 순간을 정의하는 역설은, 인공지능이 이론물리학의 경계를 확장하고 글로벌 칩 산업을 재편하며 컴퓨팅 인프라를 우주로 발사할 만큼 충분히 강력해졌으면서도, 여전히 요루바어로 적절한 의료 조언을 할 수 없을 만큼 불안정하고, 자체 창조자들이 완전히 신뢰하기엔 너무 예측 불가능하며, 미국 연구소와 중국 대기업과 자유로운 접근을 요구하는 각국 정부 사이의 지정학적 경쟁에 너무 깊이 얽혀 있어 다음 기하급수적 도약이 도래하기 전에 어떤 일관된 안전과 거버넌스의 틀도 자리 잡을 수 없다는 것이다.

grammarElements.text: The paradox defining this moment in technological history
grammarElements.label: 주어

grammarElements.text: is that
grammarElements.label: 동사 + that절

grammarElements.text: artificial intelligence has simultaneously become powerful enough to ... , yet remains too unreliable to ...
grammarElements.label: enough to + yet too ... to 대비 구조

grammarElements.text: too entangled in geopolitical rivalry ... for any coherent framework ... to take hold
grammarElements.label: too ... for ... to 구문

vocabulary.example: Governments demanding unfettered access to AI systems raise profound ethical questions.

expressions.usage: 새로운 제도나 체계가 확립되어 작동하기 시작할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: The paradox is that [subject] has become powerful enough to [A], [B], and [C], yet remains too [flaw 1] to ..., too [flaw 2] for ..., and too [flaw 3] for ...
patterns.explanation: 역량과 한계를 대칭적으로 대비시켜 핵심 역설을 집약하는 마무리 패턴
examples: The paradox is that the internet has become powerful enough to connect billions, educate millions, and democratise knowledge, yet remains too fragmented to govern, too vulnerable to secure, and too addictive for society to manage.

drillQuestions.question: AI remains too entangled in geopolitical rivalry for any coherent framework of safety and governance to take ______ before the next leap.

sentences.notes: 기사 전체를 하나의 역설로 집약하는 결론부이다. powerful enough to의 세 성과와 too ... to의 세 한계가 대칭 구조를 이룬다.

koreanAlignment.en: The paradox defining this moment in technological history
koreanAlignment.ko: 기술 역사에서 이 순간을 정의하는 역설

koreanAlignment.en: powerful enough to expand the frontiers of theoretical physics
koreanAlignment.ko: 이론물리학의 경계를 확장할 만큼 충분히 강력한

koreanAlignment.en: too unreliable to give competent medical advice in Yoruba
koreanAlignment.ko: 요루바어로 적절한 의료 조언을 할 수 없을 만큼 불안정한

koreanAlignment.en: too entangled in geopolitical rivalry for any coherent framework to take hold
koreanAlignment.ko: 어떤 일관된 틀도 자리 잡을 수 없을 만큼 지정학적 경쟁에 얽힌
